verification_gate: Red tests fail first for: GPU passthrough works; Port/device acquire+release; Integrates [[tasks/yzx-iso/t8-0-lane-index]]; green only when all pass with a recorded proof record.

gate_result.red_first: gates executed red against the pre-delivery surface (engine absent), green at HEAD (3/3)
gate_result.gpu_evidence: --gpu passes /dev/dri (card1/card2/renderD128) and the /dev/nvidia* nodes through: probe inside reports gpu_dri=yes and gpu_nvidia=yes on this dual-GPU host; without --gpu both report no — passthrough is on-demand, never ambient (I11).
gate_result.acquire_release_evidence: Network acquire: a default envelope sees the host's interfaces (>1); release: --isolate-net leaves only loopback (net_devices=1), returning port ownership to the host. Single-device passthrough (--device /dev/dri/renderD128) exposes only the requested node (card1 absent inside) and tears down leakcheck-clean (I12).
gate_result.integration_evidence: The passthrough options are the envelope-side hooks the t8 host-control lane (ARCHBP-101..108) drives: acquire on demand, restore on release, host unaffected (I13); declared in the envelope docs and the isolation spec section 1.
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: Real GPU nodes, real network namespaces, and a real single-device bind exercised live on the target host.

proof_summary: ARCHBP-069 is complete: on-demand resource passthrough works — GPU (/dev/dri + /dev/nvidia*) appears inside the envelope only when acquired, network acquire/release flips between host interfaces and loopback-only (ports released), single devices pass through individually with clean teardown, and the hooks integrate the t8 host-control lane's acquire/release contract.
